title: Diagnose and fix intermittent user authentication failure
description: Users are intermittently unable to log in due to an unidentified authentication error that surfaces under specific conditions. The issue affects a subset of login attempts and is likely related to session token handling or JWT validation logic. Resolving this will restore reliable access and eliminate the error from production logs.
acceptance_criteria: A user with valid credentials can complete login without authentication errors under normal and peak load
The root cause is identified and documented in the fix PR description
No regression in the existing authentication and session test suite
The POST /auth/login error rate drops to zero in production monitoring after deploy

title: Implement user profile view and edit page
description: Users currently have no way to view or update their display name and account settings within TundraBoard. A profile page will allow users to manage their personal information and review account details. This feature is a prerequisite for future personalisation capabilities such as notification preferences and avatar upload.
acceptance_criteria: Users can view their display name and email on the profile page when authenticated
Users can update their display name and the change persists after page reload
Email address is read-only on the profile page and requires a separate verified change flow
The profile page is accessible from the main navigation menu for all authenticated users
Input validation shows appropriate error messages for empty or overly-long display names

title: Investigate and resolve slow workspace dashboard load times
description: The workspace dashboard endpoint is experiencing high latency, with p95 response times exceeding 3 seconds for workspaces containing many projects. Profiling indicates sequential per-project database queries (N+1 pattern) are the primary cause. Batching queries, parallelising independent lookups, and adding appropriate indexes will bring p95 response time within the 200ms target.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard endpoint p95 latency is below 200ms measured in staging with 30 concurrent projects
The number of database queries per dashboard request is fixed and does not scale with project count
A load test covering workspaces with 10, 30, and 50 projects passes without exceeding the latency target
Performance changes are covered by integration tests that verify correct data is returned at each project scale

title: Audit and update outdated npm dependencies to current stable versions
description: Several npm dependencies are outdated and may contain known vulnerabilities or compatibility issues with current Node.js versions. Running a dependency audit and updating to the latest stable releases will reduce security exposure and keep the project aligned with supported package versions. This is routine maintenance to be performed as a single batch update with full test validation.
acceptance_criteria: npm audit --audit-level=high reports zero high or critical vulnerabilities after the update
All existing tests pass after the dependency update with no modifications to test code
Any breaking changes in updated packages are addressed and documented in the PR description
The updated package-lock.json is committed and the CI pipeline passes on the new lockfile

title: Add unit test coverage for createTask, updateTask, searchTasks, and deleteTask
description: The taskService module currently lacks unit tests for its core exported functions, creating a gap in the regression safety net that has already allowed bugs to reach production. Adding targeted tests for each CRUD operation will prevent future regressions and document the expected behaviour of the service layer. Tests should follow the existing Vitest + vi.mock pattern established in the project.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests exist for createTask, updateTask, getTask, searchTasks, and deleteTask
Each test mocks prisma using the vi.mock pattern from the existing test suite
Edge cases are covered: nonexistent task ID, missing required fields, and invalid filter values in searchTasks
Statement coverage for taskService.ts reaches at least 90% as reported by vitest --coverage
Tests run in CI without requiring a live database connection

title: Optimise workspace dashboard query performance and add project pagination
description: The workspace dashboard endpoint degrades significantly for workspaces containing many projects, with load times of 8–9 seconds reported by users in production. The root causes are N+1 database queries per project and missing pagination, causing unbounded result sets. Introducing query batching, parallel execution of independent queries, and cursor-based pagination will reduce latency to an acceptable range for workspaces of any size.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard endpoint loads in under 500ms for workspaces with up to 50 projects as measured in a staging load test
Project list supports cursor-based pagination with a configurable page size defaulting to 20
Database query count per dashboard request is bounded and does not grow with the number of projects
A regression test is added to CI that fails if p95 latency exceeds 500ms under simulated load with 50 projects

title: Fix user login authentication error preventing account access
description: Users are encountering an authentication error when attempting to log in, preventing successful sign-in and blocking access to their workspaces. The issue must be diagnosed and resolved to restore normal access for affected users. This appears to be a bug in the login or session initialisation flow requiring immediate investigation.
acceptance_criteria: Users with valid credentials can log in successfully without receiving authentication errors
The authentication error no longer appears in production error logs after the fix is deployed
A test case that reproduces the original error condition is added and passes after the fix
No regression in existing login, session, and token validation tests

title: Clarification required — task request does not contain actionable information
description: The submitted task request does not contain sufficient information to create a meaningful work item. A task must specify the affected feature or system, the problem or change required, and the expected outcome. Please revise the request with these details before the task is created.
acceptance_criteria: Task owner provides a revised description specifying the affected system or feature
Revised description includes a clear expected outcome or definition of done
Revised task is reviewed and approved before entering the backlog

title: Investigate and fix intermittent login failure affecting a subset of users
description: Some users are intermittently unable to log in, likely due to a bug in the authentication flow that surfaces under specific conditions such as session expiry or concurrent requests. The issue has been reported multiple times and needs to be reproduced in a controlled environment, root-caused, and fixed. Once resolved, the fix must be verified against the existing test suite and monitored in production to confirm the error rate returns to zero.
acceptance_criteria: The intermittent login failure is consistently reproducible in a staging environment with documented reproduction steps
Root cause is identified, explained, and documented in the PR description
A fix is deployed and the login error rate in production returns to the pre-regression baseline
At least one regression test is added that would have caught the original issue

title: Security review: assess task input pipeline for prompt injection vulnerabilities
description: User-supplied task titles and descriptions are processed by AI-assisted features, creating potential exposure to prompt injection if inputs are not properly sanitised before being passed to language models. A focused security review should assess whether task content can influence model behaviour beyond its intended scope. Findings should drive hardening of the input handling pipeline and the addition of guardrail tests.
acceptance_criteria: All user-supplied input paths to AI-assisted features are identified and documented
A sanitisation or guardrail layer is implemented and tested for each identified path
The security review findings are documented and any high-severity issues tracked as separate tasks
The hardened pipeline is validated against a set of at least 10 known injection patterns
Zero injection attempts in the test set result in unintended model behaviour after hardening
